title: SENIOR COMPANION PROGRAM - 211info
text: CLACKAMAS COUNTY SOCIAL SERVICES SENIOR COMPANION PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides volunteers to help with shopping, providing rides, and companionship. The program supports independence through shared activities, transportation, and companionship. Also provides short periods of relief to primary caregivers. Volunteer opportunities available. If income-eligible, volunteers may receive a small stipend plus mileage reimbursement. 2051 Kaen Road #135 Oregon City, OR 97045 Eligibility: Program participants: Homebound older adults and adults with disabilities who meet all the following: Living independently in their home Ambulatory (cane or walker under 10 lbs.), no wheelchair or scooter bound Need transportation at least once a week Looking for weekly companionship and support Volunteers: Age 55 and older Hours: Phone: Monday-Friday 7am-6pm Email (503) 655-8875 Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers Message Phone (503) 655-8875 Email Address: [email protected] INTAKE PROCEDURE: Call for information FEES: None DOCUMENTS: Call for information LANGUAGES: English, Spanish, access to language line AREA SERVED: Clackamas County SITE HOURS: Monday-Thursday 7am-6pm TRAVEL/LOCATION INFORMATION: Located at the intersection of Kaen and Beavercreek Roads, at the top of the hill in Oregon City. On TriMet Bus Line 33 with sign: Clackamas Community College/CCC Services: Age Peer Networking Volunteer Opportunities Other Services Offered At This Location: COMMUNITY GROUPS AND GOVERNMENT/ADMINISTRATIVE OFFICES - ADMINISTRATION COMMUNITY GROUPS AND GOVERNMENT/ADMINISTRATIVE OFFICES - AREA AGENCY ON AGING HEALTH SUPPORTIVE SERVICES - AGING AND DISABILITY RESOURCE CONNECTION HEALTH SUPPORTIVE SERVICES - SENIOR HEALTH INSURANCE BENEFITS ASSISTANCE INDIVIDUAL AND FAMILY SUPPORT SERVICES - OPTIONS COUNSELING INDIVIDUAL AND FAMILY SUPPORT SERVICES - OREGON PROJECT INDEPENDENCE–MEDICAID INFORMATION SERVICES - FAMILY CAREGIVER SUPPORT PROGRAM AND RELATIVES AS PARENTS REHABILITATION/HABILITATION SERVICES - DEVELOPMENTAL DISABILITIES TRANSPORTATION - TRANSPORTATION REACHING PEOPLE UTILITIES - UTILITY SUPPORT If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service.
